source_jobs.title: Global Growth Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Global Growth Lead at Passage, you will be responsible for driving growth through our network of recruitment partners across the world. You will manage a portfolio of high-performing agencies, helping them scale their student recruitment to Canada while ensuring every student they send succeeds from application to arrival. This role is ideal for a results-driven B2B account manager with deep experience in the study abroad industry, especially within the Canadian admissions and visa ecosystem.




WHO WE ARE

Our mission is bold but simple: by 2030, we aim to dismantle the barriers preventing brilliant minds from accessing the opportunities they deserve. Starting with financial barriers, we provide loans to international students and refugees so they can pursue higher education in Canada and unlock a world of possibilities.




WHO YOU ARE

 - Mission-Driven: You believe education can change lives and want your work to create impact

 - Results-Oriented: You thrive on clear targets and deliver measurable outcomes

 - Relationship-Focused: You build trust quickly and sustain long-term partnerships

 - Tech-Savvy: You leverage CRMs, dashboards, and digital tools to drive efficiency

 - Excellence-Obsessed: You take pride in delivering quality and consistency
   
   


WHAT YOU WILL DO

Partner Management

 - Manage and grow relationships with global recruitment agents to maximize enrollments

 - Serve as the main point of contact, providing proactive advisory and operational support

 - Increase Passage’s market share through partner engagement, training, and enablement

 - Ensure partners adopt and effectively use Passage’s digital tools and workflows

 - Uphold compliance and ethical standards across all partner activities

Sales & Growth Management

 - Own and deliver on intake-wise sales and enrollment targets

 - Develop and execute partner activation plans—visits, training sessions, marketing campaigns, and student events

 - Monitor partner performance through CRM analytics and identify growth opportunities

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with internal admissions, marketing, and finance teams to drive outcomes
   
   


WHAT YOU BRING

 - Experience: 3+ years of proven success in partner or account management within the study abroad / international education sector

 - Expertise: Deep understanding of Canadian admissions, visa, and student recruitment cycles

 - Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Marketing, or a related field
   
   


SKILLS

 - Exceptional communication and stakeholder management

 - Strong analytical and reporting skills

 - Proven ability to translate strategy into action

 - Highly organized with attention to detail and follow-through

 - Professional presence and integrity in handling confidential information
   
   


WHY JOIN PASSAGE

At Passage, we’re redefining global student mobility by combining financing, admissions, and career success into one seamless experience. You will join a fast-growing, mission-driven team that’s helping students access opportunities that change lives.

source_jobs.title: Principal Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Who we are: We build technology that removes barriers blocking talent from opportunity, combining the latest advancements in AI with human judgment.

How we expand access: Access isn't real if it's unaffordable. Through our sister company, Passage Funding, we provide financing for international students.

What we believe: Access to opportunity should not be determined by geography, gender, or wealth. We reinvest a portion of our profits to support those cut off from education - including our Afghan program.

Access is the product: We build AI that turns paperwork into decisions, and decisions into real outcomes.

Our mission is bold but simple: by 2030, we aim to dismantle the barriers preventing brilliant minds from accessing the opportunities they deserve.




THE ROLE

We are looking for a Principal Software Engineer to help architect and scale the core systems that power Passage.

This is a senior technical leadership role focused on building reliable, scalable systems while shaping the long-term technical direction of the company. You will work closely with engineering, product, and leadership teams to design systems that support Passage’s AI-driven platform and global operations.

You will take ownership of complex technical problems, define architectural standards, and help ensure our systems remain reliable, secure, and scalable as the company grows.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys solving deep technical problems while also influencing engineering culture and technical strategy across the organization.



WHAT YOU WILL DO




ARCHITECT CORE SYSTEMS

 - Design and evolve the architecture of Passage’s core platform

 - Build scalable backend services that support admissions automation, financial systems, and operational workflows

 - Ensure systems are reliable, secure, and capable of supporting global scale


LEAD COMPLEX TECHNICAL INITIATIVES

 - Own the design and implementation of large engineering initiatives

 - Break down complex problems into clear technical solutions

 - Guide teams through major architectural decisions


ESTABLISH ENGINEERING STANDARDS

 - Define best practices for system design, testing, performance, and reliability

 - Improve engineering workflows and development processes

 - Ensure high-quality, maintainable code across the platform


PARTNER ACROSS THE COMPANY

 - Work closely with product, data, and operations teams to understand business needs

 - Translate product requirements into scalable technical solutions

 - Help leadership understand technical trade-offs and long-term architecture decisions


MENTOR AND ELEVATE THE ENGINEERING TEAM

 - Mentor engineers across the organization

 - Review system designs and provide technical guidance

 - Help cultivate a high-ownership, high-quality engineering culture




QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering, or a related technical field

 - 5+ years of professional software engineering experience

 - Deep experience designing and building scalable backend systems

 - Strong programming skills in modern backend languages (such as Python, TypeScript, Go, or similar)

 - Experience working with distributed systems and cloud infrastructure

 - Strong system design and architectural thinking

 - Experience leading complex technical initiatives across multiple teams

 - Ability to operate independently and make high-impact technical decisions in a fast-moving environment
   


NICE TO HAVE

 - Proficient in Python, Django, TypeScript, and React.

 - Experience with cloud platforms such as GCP is a plus.

 - Deep knowledge of software design patterns and architecture.

 - Strong understanding of database design and development.

 - Bachelor degree or higher in computer science or related field




LOCATION

Passage operates with an in-office working model.

Candidates must be based in Toronto or open to relocating and working five days per week in our Toronto office.




READY TO JOIN US?

We are

source_jobs.title: Global Acquisition Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Global Acquisition Coordinator at Passage, you will be responsible for driving growth by signing up and activating new recruitment partners across the world. You will manage a mandate of acquiring high-performing agencies, activating them on the Passage platform. This role is ideal for a results-driven B2B sales manager with deep experience in the study abroad industry.




WHO WE ARE

Our mission is bold but simple: by 2030, we aim to dismantle the barriers preventing brilliant minds from accessing the opportunities they deserve. Starting with financial barriers, we provide loans to international students and refugees so they can pursue higher education in Canada and unlock a world of possibilities.




WHO YOU ARE

 - Mission-Driven: You believe education can change lives and want your work to create impact

 - Results-Oriented: You thrive on clear targets and deliver measurable outcomes

 - Growth-Focused: You are obsessed about driving scale quickly and efficiently

 - Tech-Savvy: You leverage CRMs, dashboards, and digital tools to drive performance against targets

 - Excellence-Obsessed: You take pride in delivering quality and consistency
   
   


WHAT YOU WILL DO

Partner Acquisition

 - Discover, promote and sign up global recruitment agents to the Passage platform

 - Increase Passage’s market penetration in the covered geographies

 - Serve as the main point of contact, providing training and initial operational support

 - Ensure partners adopt Passage’s digital tools and workflows
   Uphold compliance and KYC standards for signed up partners

Activation Management

 - Own and deliver on quarterly activation targets

 - Develop and execute partner acquisition and activation plans—visits, training sessions, marketing campaigns, and industry events

 - Monitor partner activation through CRM analytics and identify growth opportunities

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with internal sales, admissions, and marketing teams to drive outcomes
   
   


WHAT YOU BRING

 - Experience: 3+ years of proven success in partner or account management within the study abroad / international education sector

 - Expertise: Deep understanding of Canadian admissions, visa, and student recruitment cycles

 - Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Marketing, or a related field




SKILLS

 - Exceptional communication and stakeholder management

 - Strong analytical and reporting skills

 - Proven ability to translate strategy into action

 - Highly organized with attention to detail and follow-through

 - Professional presence and integrity in handling confidential information




WHY JOIN PASSAGE

At Passage, we’re redefining global student mobility by combining financing, admissions, and career success into one seamless experience. You will join a fast-growing, mission-driven team that’s helping students access opportunities that change lives.

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Who we are: We build technology that removes barriers blocking talent from opportunity, combining the latest advancements in AI with human judgment.

How we expand access: Access isn't real if it's unaffordable. Through our sister company, Passage Funding, we provide financing for international students.

What we believe: Access to opportunity should not be determined by geography, gender, or wealth. We reinvest a portion of our profits to support those cut off from education - including our Afghan program.

Access is the product: We build AI that turns paperwork into decisions, and decisions into real outcomes.

Our mission is bold but simple: by 2030, we aim to dismantle the barriers preventing brilliant minds from accessing the opportunities they deserve.




WHO YOU ARE

You’re a mission-driven, ambitious individual who wants to win by doing good. You’re passionately curious about the world, empathetic toward others, and driven to create meaningful impact. You’re not afraid of a problem and see it as an opportunity. You’re a builder. 

You likely have a strength in either frontend or backend development. If you're more experienced in frontend, we value your eye for design and your talent for creating engaging user experiences. If backend is your area of expertise, we look for your ability to architect complex services and manage databases effectively. In both cases, we count on you to contribute thoughtfully to product decisions.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Solve complex problems end-to-end.

 - Design, develop, and maintain complex software applications.

 - Lead the technical design and architecture of software systems.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams to develop new features and products.

 - Write clean, maintainable code in accordance with best practices.

 - Participate in code reviews and provide feedback to improve overall code quality.

 - Exhibit a strong sense of ownership in all aspects of your work.




NICE TO HAVES

 - 8+ years of varying experiences as a Software Engineer.

 - Proficient in Python, Django, TypeScript, and React.

 - Experience with cloud platforms such as GCP is a plus.

 - Deep knowledge of software design patterns and architecture.

 - Strong understanding of database design and development.

 - Bachelor degree or higher  in computer science or related field
   


READY TO JOIN US?

We are building a company designed to expand access to life-changing opportunities around the world.

If you are excited about building systems, solving complex problems, and shaping how a company makes decisions, we would love to meet you.

Note: Artificial intelligence may be used in the screening or assessment of applicants.

If your experience does not match every requirement but you believe you can thrive in this role, we encourage you to apply.


Compensation

C$150K to C$200K + Offers Equity

source_jobs.title: Director of Finance
source_jobs.description:
DIRECTOR OF FINANCE


TORONTO, IN-OFFICE | HIGH OWNERSHIP | BUILD FOR SCALE





ABOUT US

Who we are: We build technology that removes barriers blocking talent from opportunity, combining the latest advancements in AI with human judgment.

How we expand access: Access isn't real if it's unaffordable. Through our sister company, Passage Funding, we provide financing for international students.

What we believe: Access to opportunity should not be determined by geography, gender, or wealth. We reinvest a portion of our profits to support those cut off from education - including our Afghan program.

Access is the product: We build AI that turns paperwork into decisions, and decisions into real outcomes.

Our mission is bold but simple: by 2030, we aim to dismantle the barriers preventing brilliant minds from accessing the opportunities they deserve.




WHO YOU ARE

We’re looking for a Director of Finance with a CPA designation and a demonstrated history of owning the accounting and financial reporting function within a high-growth, VC-backed company.

You likely began your career in public accounting (Manager+ level) and have since operated as a Controller within a scaling startup environment. You understand what strong financial infrastructure looks like and have successfully built and elevated reporting processes through periods of rapid growth.

You are technically strong in IFRS and comfortable standing behind your financial statements. You are confident in leading audit processes, setting accounting policy, and ensuring financial reporting is accurate, timely, and decision-useful.

You bring experience but remain hands-on. You are willing to review reconciliations, refine close processes, strengthen reporting models, and dive into operational detail when required. You lead from the front and take full ownership of outcomes.

If you are motivated by accountability, precision, and building financial infrastructure that can support significant scale, we’d love to meet you.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

You will take full ownership of the finance and accounting function and ensure our financial foundation is built to support the next stage of growth.

 - Accounting & Financial reporting
   
   - Own the month-end, quarter-end, and year-end close processes
   
   - Prepare and oversee IFRS financial statements
   
   - Lead the external audit end-to-end
   
   - Establish and maintain robust accounting policies and documentation
   
   - Ensure accurate revenue recognition and accounting for complex transactions

 - Financial Infrastructure & Scale
   
   - Strengthen and scale financial processes to support rapid growth
   
   - Ensure integrity of core financial data and reporting systems
   
   - Oversee treasury, cash management, and working capital processes
   
   - Build scalable workflows that reduce risk and improve efficiency

 - Tax & Compliance
   
   - Oversee all corporate tax filings, including SR&ED, and compliance requirements
   
   - Coordinate with external advisors across jurisdictions

 - Reporting & Governance
   
   - Ensure board-ready financial reporting
   
   - Partner cross-functionally to uphold financial discipline across the organization
   
   - Support long-term financial readiness as the company scales




QUALIFICATIONS

 - CPA designation (required)

 - 8–12 years of progressive accounting experience

 - Manager level audit experience (Big 4 preferred)

 - Prior Controller-level ownership within a VC-backed startup

 - Proven experience scaling accounting operations through rapid growth

 - Direct ownership of IFRS financial statements and external audit

 - Strong technical accounting knowledge and practical judgment

 - Ability to operate independently in a fast-paced, high-growth environment
   
   


LOCATION

Passage operates with an in-office working model.

Candidates must be based in Toronto or open to relocating and working five days per week in our Toronto office.




READY TO JOIN US?

We

source_jobs.title: Business Operations Associate
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Imagine a world where your birthplace doesn’t limit your dreams. 🌍 That’s what Passage is here to create. We aren’t just a bridge of financial support; we connect families all around the world to life-changing opportunities. We believe in a future where deserving students can pursue their dreams & ambitions—even if their parents don’t have money.

Our mission is bold but simple: by 2030, we aim to dismantle the barriers preventing brilliant minds from accessing the opportunities they deserve. Starting with financial barriers, we’re offering loans to international students and refugees, helping them pursue better education in Canada and unlock a world of possibilities—not just for themselves, but for their families and future generations, too.

Admin: It’s important to note that we maintain an in-office working policy, and we require candidates to either reside in Toronto or be open to relocating to the area and committing to a five-day-a-week in-office schedule.




WHO YOU ARE

We're hiring a Business Operations Associate to help run and evolve the engine behind our growth. This isn’t a back-office role—this is a front-row seat to how strategy becomes execution. You’ll be deep in operational workflows one day, then in a cross-functional brainstorm the next. You’ll analyze, optimize, and help build systems that scale our mission—and our impact.

If you're early in your career, hungry to grow fast, and energized by solving complex problems with a practical mindset, this is for you. We’re looking for someone who thrives in fast-moving environments, takes initiative, and wants real ownership—fast. This is your chance to be part of something meaningful from the ground up, with a clear path to grow.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

Own and Optimize Core Workflows

 - Own operational processes including loan application processing, underwriting, diligence, and servicing.

 - Ensure systems are audit-ready, accurate, and consistently up to date.
   

Build Tools and Systems That Scale

 - Create and refine SOPs, dashboards, and internal tooling to streamline workflows and improve team productivity.

 - Identify operational bottlenecks and drive continuous improvement.
   

Collaborate Across Teams

 - Coordinate across credit, finance, and product teams to move applications from intake to decision.

 - Jump into high-priority special projects with senior leaders—strategy meets execution.




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 1–3 years of experience in business operations, consulting, finance, or a similarly demanding environment.

 - Educational background in Banking, Business Administration, Finance, Economics, or a related field.

 - Strong communication, high ownership, and a team-first mindset.

 - You want more than a role—you want momentum. You’re ambitious, proactive, and ready to own outcomes.

 - Excellent organization, communication, and energized by problem-solving.




NICE TO HAVES

 - Unique skillset in data analysis, programming, mathematics another technical field.

 - Previous experience founding a startup or initiative, showcasing entrepreneurial spirit and leadership, or having worked in a startup during early or growth stages. 



If your experience doesn’t perfectly match every requirement but you’re excited about this role and confident you can thrive in it, we encourage you to apply.

Note: Artificial intelligence may be used in the screening or assessment of applicants.

Expected annual compensation: CA $60 000 – CA $110 000.

source_jobs.title: Director of Data
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Who we are: We build technology that removes barriers blocking talent from opportunity, combining the latest advancements in AI with human judgment.

How we expand access: Access isn't real if it's unaffordable. Through our sister company, Passage Funding, we provide financing for international students.

What we believe: Access to opportunity should not be determined by geography, gender, or wealth. We reinvest a portion of our profits to support those cut off from education - including our Afghan program.

Access is the product: We build AI that turns paperwork into decisions, and decisions into real outcomes.

Our mission is bold but simple: by 2030, we aim to dismantle the barriers preventing brilliant minds from accessing the opportunities they deserve.






THE ROLE

We are looking for a Director of Data to build and own the data foundation at Passage.

This is a foundational role. You will design the systems that turn our operational data into reliable infrastructure the entire company can trust.

You are both a technical builder and a strategic thinker. You enjoy designing data systems from the ground up, turning messy operational data into structured platforms that enable better decisions across the business.

From day one, you will work closely with leadership across engineering, growth, credit, finance, product, and operations. You will design the company’s data architecture, build pipelines and analytical models, and create the metrics that guide how Passage operates.

We are a lean, high-ownership team that moves quickly. There is no fixed hierarchy and no ceiling on growth. We are looking for someone excited to build the data brain of the company, establish best practices, and eventually help grow the data team as Passage scales.




WHAT YOU WILL DO




BUILD THE DATA FOUNDATION

 - Design and own the analytics layer of Passage’s data platform

 - Develop scalable data models and transformation layers that power analytics across the company

 - Define the company’s canonical metrics and ensure consistency across teams

 - Build dashboards and analytical frameworks used by leadership to evaluate performance and make business decisions

 - Work with data engineering to ensure reliable pipelines and data availability

 - Unify data across growth, product, lending, finance, and operations into a consistent analytics layer

 - Establish standards for data quality, monitoring, and metric governance
   


POWER ANALYTICS AND DECISION-MAKING

 - Define and maintain the company’s core metrics and analytical frameworks

 - Build dashboards used by leadership to monitor lending, product, and operational performance

 - Analyze loan portfolio performance, borrower behavior, underwriting outcomes, and operational workflows

 - Translate complex datasets into clear insights that drive better decisions
   


PARTNER ACROSS THE COMPANY

 - Work closely with teams across credit, finance, product, and operations

 - Translate business questions into structured analytical solutions

 - Help teams adopt a data-driven approach to decision-making
   


SHAPE PASSAGE’S DATA STRATEGY

 - Define the long-term architecture of Passage’s data platform

 - Implement best practices for modeling, documentation, and analytics workflows

 - Lay the foundation for the future data team, including tooling and infrastructure




QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering, statistics, mathematics, or a related technical field

 - 6–10 years of experience in analytics engineering, data engineering, or advanced analytics roles

 - Deep expertise in SQL and experience working with large operational datasets
   

 - Experience designing and maintaining data warehouses and analytical data models

 - Strong experience building data pipelines and transformation workflows

 - Proficiency in Python for data processing and analysis

 - Experience working directly with business stakeholders to translate business problems

WHO WE ARE

Imagine a world where your birthplace doesn’t limit your dreams. 🌍 That’s what Passage is here to create. We aren’t just a bridge of financial support; we connect families all around the world to life-changing opportunities. Our mission is bold but simple: by 2030, we aim to dismantle the barriers preventing brilliant minds from accessing the opportunities they deserve. Starting with financial barriers, we’re offering loans to international students and refugees, helping them pursue better education in Canada and unlock a world of possibilities—not just for themselves, but for their families and future generations, too.

It’s important to note that we maintain an in-office working policy, and we require candidates to either reside in Toronto or be open to commuting into the office a few days a week.




WHO YOU ARE

You’re a mission-driven, ambitious individual who wants to win by doing good. You’re passionately curious about the world, empathetic toward others, and driven to create meaningful impact. You’re not afraid of a problem and see it as an opportunity. You’re a builder. 




PASSAGE EXCEPTIONAL TALENT POOL

If you're interested in Passage but don't see a role that fits your experience, apply here to be apart of our talent pool! Applying to this does not guarantee our team will reach out, but we will always search for interested candidates prior to reaching out to broader networks!

source_jobs.title: Staff Analytics Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Who we are: We build technology that removes barriers blocking talent from opportunity, combining the latest advancements in AI with human judgment.

How we expand access: Access isn't real if it's unaffordable. Through our sister company, Passage Funding, we provide financing for international students.

Access is the product: We build AI that turns paperwork into decisions, and decisions into real outcomes.

THE ROLE

We are looking for a Staff Analytics Engineer to lead the development of Passage’s analytics foundation and decision infrastructure.

This role sits at the intersection of engineering, analytics, and business strategy. You will design the systems that turn operational data into reliable insights used across the company.

From day one, you will work closely with leadership across engineering, growth, credit, finance, product, and operations. You will design the company’s data architecture, build pipelines and analytical models, and create the metrics that guide how Passage operates.

This is a senior role with broad influence across the company. You will architect the analytics layer of the data platform, ensure the quality and reliability of decision data, and help establish best practices for how the organization uses data to operate and grow.

As Passage scales, you will help shape the long-term data strategy and play a key role in building the analytics function.

We are a lean, high-ownership team that moves quickly. There is no fixed hierarchy and no ceiling on growth. We are looking for someone excited to build the data brain of the company, establish best practices, and eventually help grow the data team as Passage scales.

 - Improve data quality and reliability across operational systems

 - Help the company adopt a disciplined, data-driven culture

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer

Our mission is bold but simple: by 2030, we aim to dismantle the barriers preventing brilliant minds from accessing the opportunities they deserve.




WHO YOU ARE

You’re a mission-driven, ambitious individual who wants to win by doing good. You’re passionately curious about the world, empathetic toward others, and driven to create meaningful impact. You’re not afraid of a problem and see it as an opportunity. You’re a builder. 

You likely have a strength in either frontend or backend development. If you're more experienced in frontend, we value your eye for design and your talent for creating engaging user experiences. If backend is your area of expertise, we look for your ability to architect complex services and manage databases effectively. In both cases, we count on you to contribute thoughtfully to product decisions.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Solve complex problems end-to-end.

 - Design, develop, and maintain complex software applications.

 - Lead the technical design and architecture of software systems.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams to develop new features and products.

 - Write clean, maintainable code in accordance with best practices.

 - Participate in code reviews and provide feedback to improve overall code quality.

 - Exhibit a strong sense of ownership in all aspects of your work.




NICE TO HAVES

 - 5+ years of varying experiences as a Software Engineer.

 - Proficient in Python, Django, TypeScript, and React.

 - Experience with cloud platforms such as GCP is a plus.

 - Deep knowledge of software design patterns and architecture.

 - Strong understanding of database design and development.

 - Bachelor degree or higher  in computer science or related field


LOCATION

Passage operates with an in-office working model.

Candidates must be based in Toronto or open to relocating and working five days per week in our Toronto office.




READY TO JOIN US?

We are building a company designed to expand access to life-changing opportunities around the world.


If you are excited about building systems, solving complex problems, and shaping how a company makes decisions, we would love to meet you.


Note: Artificial intelligence may be used in the screening or assessment of applicants.

If your experience does not match every requirement but you believe you can thrive in this role, we encourage you to apply.


Compensation

C$100K to C$150K + Offers Equity

source_jobs.title: Global Growth Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Global Growth Lead at Passage, you will be responsible for driving growth through our network of recruitment partners across the world. You will manage a portfolio of high-performing agencies, helping them scale their student recruitment to Canada while ensuring every student they send succeeds from application to arrival. This role is ideal for a results-driven B2B account manager with deep experience in the study abroad industry, especially within the Canadian admissions and visa ecosystem.




WHO WE ARE

Our mission is bold but simple: by 2030, we aim to dismantle the barriers preventing brilliant minds from accessing the opportunities they deserve. Starting with financial barriers, we provide loans to international students and refugees so they can pursue higher education in Canada and unlock a world of possibilities.




WHO YOU ARE

 - Mission-Driven: You believe education can change lives and want your work to create impact

 - Results-Oriented: You thrive on clear targets and deliver measurable outcomes

 - Relationship-Focused: You build trust quickly and sustain long-term partnerships

 - Tech-Savvy: You leverage CRMs, dashboards, and digital tools to drive efficiency

 - Excellence-Obsessed: You take pride in delivering quality and consistency
   
   


WHAT YOU WILL DO

Partner Management

 - Manage and grow relationships with global recruitment agents to maximize enrollments

 - Serve as the main point of contact, providing proactive advisory and operational support

 - Increase Passage’s market share through partner engagement, training, and enablement

 - Ensure partners adopt and effectively use Passage’s digital tools and workflows

 - Uphold compliance and ethical standards across all partner activities

Sales & Growth Management

 - Own and deliver on intake-wise sales and enrollment targets

 - Develop and execute partner activation plans—visits, training sessions, marketing campaigns, and student events

 - Monitor partner performance through CRM analytics and identify growth opportunities

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with internal admissions, marketing, and finance teams to drive outcomes
   
   


WHAT YOU BRING

 - Experience: 3+ years of proven success in partner or account management within the study abroad / international education sector

 - Expertise: Deep understanding of Canadian admissions, visa, and student recruitment cycles

 - Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Marketing, or a related field
   
   


SKILLS

 - Exceptional communication and stakeholder management

 - Strong analytical and reporting skills

 - Proven ability to translate strategy into action

 - Highly organized with attention to detail and follow-through

 - Professional presence and integrity in handling confidential information
   
   


WHY JOIN PASSAGE

At Passage, we’re redefining global student mobility by combining financing, admissions, and career success into one seamless experience. You will join a fast-growing, mission-driven team that’s helping students access opportunities that change lives.

source_jobs.title: Senior Accountant / Finance Manager
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Imagine a world where your birthplace doesn’t limit your dreams. That’s what Passage is here to create. We aren’t just a bridge of financial support; we connect families all around the world to life-changing opportunities. We believe in a future where deserving students can pursue their dreams and ambitions - even if their parents don’t have money.

Our mission is bold but simple: by 2030, we aim to dismantle the barriers preventing brilliant minds from accessing the opportunities they deserve. Starting with financial barriers, we’re offering loans to international students and refugees, helping them pursue better education in Canada and unlock a world of possibilities - not just for themselves, but for their families and future generations, too.




WHO YOU ARE

We’re looking for an exceptional early-career finance professional with a CPA designation and 3–5 years of progressive experience in accounting and finance. You’ll work directly with our Head of Finance and take meaningful ownership of reporting, operations, and strategic decision-making.

This is a rare opportunity to join a leading AI company in a high-impact role. From day one, you’ll lead core accounting and financial reporting processes while gaining exposure to FP&A, portfolio analytics, and corporate development. You’ll play a key role in building the financial infrastructure that supports our long-term growth.

We operate as a lean, high-performing team - there’s no fixed hierarchy or ceiling on growth. We’re looking for someone who wants to grow quickly into a Controller role and is eager to take full ownership of financial operations as the company scales. If you thrive in unstructured environments and are hungry to accelerate your career, we’d love to meet you.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

You’ll take full ownership of core accounting and financial operations, with exposure across reporting, audit, internal controls, and cross-functional initiatives that support our product and growth.



ACCOUNTING & FINANCIAL REPORTING

 - Lead day-to-day accounting operations and month-end close

 - Prepare monthly financial statements under IFRS and support external audit

 - Maintain and document accounting policies, memos, and reconciliations

 - Build and scale internal controls, systems, and reporting infrastructure


FINANCE OPERATIONS

 - Design and manage finance-related processes integrated with our product

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams to automate manual workflows


STRATEGIC REPORTING & ANALYSIS

 - Partner with the Head of Finance on investor and board reporting

 - Support strategic decision-making while preparing and maintaining critical source-of-truth datasets across accounting, operations, and lending

 - Build tools and frameworks to track KPIs, unit economics, and cash flow

You’ll also be a key contributor to long-term projects like audit readiness, financial model development, and future financing events - with increasing ownership based on performance.




QUALIFICATIONS

 - CPA designation with 3–5 years of experience, ideally starting in Big 4 audit

 - Strong understanding of accounting principles and financial reporting under IFRS

 - Comfort working independently in Excel and building reporting from scratch
   
   


NICE TO HAVES

 - Experience in financial services, lending, or structured credit

 - Exposure to FP&A, financial modelling, or investor reporting

 - Experience building and/or testing internal controls

 - Familiarity with data visualization tools (e.g. Power BI, Google Studio)
   
   


READY TO JOIN US?

We’re on a mission to make the world a fairer place, one student at a time. If you’re excited to work hard, innovate fearlessly, and collaborate with a high-energy team striving for global impact, we’d love to meet you.

Note: This role is based in Toronto, and requires a five-day-a-week in-office commitment. Applicants must be willing to relocate if they’re not already in the area.

Let’s help shape a future

notes: Passage is a fintech company focused on education financing, with a mix of tech and non-tech roles. The company has a strong mission-driven culture and is likely a startup.
